item.title: Oral Exam Readings, pt. 1
item.description:
Below, you'll find everything I read this monthish (mid-June to today) for my oral exam this winter:

Title Author Year
The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin Benjamin Franklin 1791
The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym of Nantucket Edgar Allan Poe 1838
V1 Introduction Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V1 Social Condition of the Anglo-Americans Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V1 How It Can Be Strictly Said that the People Govern in the United States Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V1 What are the real advantages which American society derives from a democratic government Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V1 Tyranny of the majority Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V1 Causes which mitigate the tyranny of the majority Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V1 The present and probable future condition of the three races that inhabit the territory of the United States Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 Philosophical method of the Americans Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 The example of the Americans does not prove that a democratic people can have no aptitude and no taste for science, literature, or art Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 Why the Americans are more addicted to practical than theoretical science Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 Literary characteristics of democratic times Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 The trade of literature Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 How American democracy has modified the English language Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 Of some sources of poetry among democratic nations Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 Influence of democracy on the feelings of Americans Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 How democracy affects the relations among masters and servants Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 Education of young women in the United States Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
V2 General survey of the subject of the influence of democratic ideas and feelings on political society Alexis de Tocqueville 1840
Murders in the Rue Morgue Edgar Allan Poe 1841
Self-Reliance Ralph Waldo Emerson 1841
Experience Ralph Waldo Emerson 1844
Nominalist and Realist Ralph Waldo Emerson 1844
Woman in the Nineteenth Century (Norton selections) Margaret Fuller 1845
Man of the Crowd Edgar Allan Poe 1850
The Uses of Great Men (from "Representative Men") Ralph Waldo Emerson 1850
Narrative of Sojourner Truth: A Northern Slave Sojourner Truth 1850
The House of the Seven Gables Nathaniel Hawthorne 1851
Uncle Tom's Cabin Harriet Beecher Stowe 1852
The Blithedale Romance Nathaniel Hawthorne 1852
Bartleby, The Scrivener: A Story of Wall-Street Herman Melville 1853
Clotel: Or, the President's Daughter William Wells Brown 1853
Speech on Kansas-Nebraska Act Abraham Lincoln 1854
Ruth Hall Fanny Fern 1854
Walden Henry David Thoreau 1854
My Bondage and My Freedom Frederick Douglass 1855
"House Divided" Speech Abraham Lincoln 1858
First Douglas Debate Abraham Lincoln 1858
On Liberty J.S. Mill 1859
202 (185) (" 'Faith' is a fine invention") Emily Dickinson 1861
225 (199) ("I'm 'wife' - I've finished that - ") Emily Dickinson 1861
259 (287) ("A Clock stopped -") Emily Dickinson 1861
260 (288) ("I'm Nobody! Who are you?") Emily Dickinson 1861
269 (249) ("Wild nights - Wild nights!") Emily Dickinson 1861
Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl Harriet Jacobs 1861
Walking Henry David Thoreau 1861
Life in the Iron Mills Rebecca Harding Davis 1861
The Morgesons Elizabeth Stoddard 1862
314 (254) (" 'Hope' is the thing with feathers -") Emily Dickinson 1862
320 (258) ("There's a certain Slant of light") Emily Dickinson 1862
340 (280) ("I felt a Funeral, in my Brain") Emily Dickinson 1862
372 (341) ("After great pain, a formal feeling comes - ") Emily Dickinson 1862
383 (585) ("I like to see it lap the Miles -") Emily Dickinson 1862
409 (303) ("The Soul selects her own Society -") Emily Dickinson 1862
411 (528) ("Mine - by the Right of the White Election!") Emily Dickinson 1862
445 (613) ("They shut me up in Prose -") Emily Dickinson 1862
446 (448) ("This was a Poet - It is That") Emily Dickinson 1862
466 (657) ("I dwell in Possibility - ") Emily Dickinson 1862
479 (712) ("Because I could not stop for Death -") Emily Dickinson 1862
533 (569) ("I reckon - When I count at all -") Emily Dickinson 1863
591 (465) ("I heard a Fly buzz - when I died -") Emily Dickinson 1863
620 (435) ("Much Madness is divinest Sense -") Emily Dickinson 1863
764 (754) ("My Life had stood - a Loaded Gun -") Emily Dickinson 1863
1096 (986) ("A narrow Fellow in the Grass") Emily Dickinson 1865
Miss Ravenel's Conversion from Secession to Loyalty John W. de Forest 1867
Little Women Louisa May Alcott 1868
The Subjection of Women J.S. Mill 1869
1263 (1129) ("Tell all the Truth but tell it slant -") Emily Dickinson 1872
An Autumn Holiday Sarah Orne Jewett 1880
Introduction to A Century of Dishonor Helen Hunt Jackson 1881
1577 (1545) ("The Bible is an antique Volume -") Emily Dickinson 1882
The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn Mark Twain 1884
The Rise of Silas Lapham William Dean Howells 1885
1788 (1763) ("Fame is a bee") Emily Dickinson 1886
Looking Backward, 2000—1887 Edward Bellamy 1888
How the Other Half Lives Jacob Riis 1890
A Hazard of New Fortunes William Dean Howells 1890

item.title: Hello World
item.description:
Thanks for visiting my new site!

Please check out the About and CV pages for more information about me.
